cars.description: Spec open-wheel trainer: no downforce, momentum-driven, and unforgiving of mistakes. The classic school for racecraft and throttle control.
Small, punchy spec car; huge grids and constant three-wide short-track chaos.
Featherweight track toy with huge grip-to-power ratio; a favorite for hot-lapping and learning downforce.
Momentum-first spec racer; carry speed or lose everything.
The ground-effect pioneer of 1978 — narrow tires, manual gearbox, and pure vintage skill.
Naturally aspirated club racer — simple, honest handling for learning race pace.
Winged 410 sprint car on dirt — throttle-steer and slide the thing sideways to go fast.
The updated global-spec MX-5, the most-raced road car in iRacing. Huge grids, tiny gaps, and a masterclass in tire management.
The IndyCar chassis — ovals and road/street courses, extreme commitment, and aero-sensitive in traffic.

cars.carName: NASCAR Cup Series Chevrolet Camaro ZL1
Lucas Oil Off Road Pro 2 Truck

cars.description: High-downforce open-wheeler bridging junior formula and the top classes.
FIA F3 car — the step between F4 and F2, with real downforce and DRS-free racecraft.

cars.carName: Lucas Oil Off Road Pro 4 Truck

cars.description: Trucks race close and rough; superb for learning oval pack craft.

cars.carName: Lucas Oil Off Road Pro 2 Lite

cars.description: Mid-engined GT4 — friendly balance and strong brakes; the definitive GT4 benchmark.
V10 GT3 that's nimble in transitions but can snap if you overslip the rear.

cars.carName: NASCAR Cup Series Next Gen Chevrolet Camaro ZL1
NASCAR Cup Series Next Gen Ford Mustang
NASCAR Cup Series Next Gen Toyota Camry

cars.description: Twin-turbo V8 GT3 with responsive turn-in; the classic modern Ferrari GT.
TCR sedan known for a stable front end and strong braking.
Front-drive TCR hatch; manage wheelspin out of slow corners and use the curbs hard.
The global junior single-seater: modest power, real aero, and the first rung of the FIA ladder. Teaches precision without punishing every slip.
Muscular V8 GT4; strong traction and a planted rear.

cars.carName: NASCAR Truck Toyota Tundra TRD Pro

cars.description: Front-engined GT3 with a wide, stable operating window; forgiving and consistent across most circuits.
Approachable GT4 with a wide setup window — great first GT car.
Affordable rear-drive coupe on Michelin tires; a natural step up from the MX-5 with a livelier rear end.
Modern F1-spec car: extreme downforce, hybrid power, and brutal braking performance.
LMP3 prototype; the most accessible intro to downforce racing.
Hybrid GTP prototype — top class of iRacing sports-car endurance, with energy deployment to manage.
Rear-engined GT3 with class-leading traction out of slow corners; rewards a smooth, patient throttle.
V6 twin-turbo GT3 praised for strong traction and a friendly aero platform — a popular all-rounder in GT3 fields.
Hybrid GTP prototype; heavy braking-energy management and immense mid-corner grip.

cars.carName: Audi R8 LMS EVO II GT3
cars.description: Naturally aspirated V10 GT3 — planted, predictable, and easy to place on the road.

cars.carName: Dirt Outlaw Micro Sprint Car - Winged
Dirt Outlaw Micro Sprint Car - Non-Winged

cars.description: American V8 GT3 with strong straight-line pace and a stable rear under braking.
V8 GT3 with a distinctive on-throttle rotation; strong on point-and-squirt circuits.
Mid-engined GT3 with quick change of direction and light, precise steering.
